questions.prompt: What are the three components of the "Three-Part Contingency"?
Which term describes learning through consequences?
If a behavior increases because a stimulus was added following the behavior, this is:
What occurs when a previously reinforced behavior is no longer reinforced, leading to a temporary increase in the behavior?
Which schedule of reinforcement provides a reward after an average number of responses?
Reinforcement is delivered on average every 2 minutes in which the behaviors occur. What reinforcement schedule is this?
Which antecedent increases or decreases the value of a consequence?
Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is officially classified as a:
The "Triad of Impairments" in Autism includes difficulties in which areas?
options: Social communication, social interaction, and restricted/repetitive behaviors

questions.prompt: What is "Joint Attention"?
options: Paying attention to two tasks at once
Sharing focus on an object or event with another person

questions.prompt: Which of these is a common co-occurring (comorbid) condition with ASD?
Antecedent interventions are considered:
The Premack Principle is commonly referred to as:
A teacher asks a student to perform three easy tasks before a hard task. This is:
What acronym is used to remember the four functions of behavior?
Which function involves engaging in behavior to get away from a task?
If a child rocks back and forth because it "feels good," the function is likely:
What is the primary purpose of a Functional Behavior Assessment (FBA)?
options: To identify the purpose or function of a behavior

questions.prompt: An Operational Definition must be:
options: Focused on a child’s internal motives

questions.prompt: A "Replacement Behavior" should:
options: Be more difficult to perform than the problem behavior
Serve the same function as the challenging behavior
Be unrelated to the original behavior
Only be taught after the problem behavior stops

questions.prompt: Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA) involves:
options: Reinforcing any behavior other than the problem behavior
Reinforcing a specific appropriate behavior that serves the same function

questions.prompt: Reinforcement delivered on a timer regardless of behavior is:
You reinforce the client whenever they are NOT engaged in verbal stereotypy. What type of differential reinforcement is this?
This signals that reinforcement is available:
Presenting the same instruction (SD) repeatedly in a row is called:
Any behavior the interventionist engages in that increases the likelihood that the learner will respond correctly is known as:
Which prompting procedure starts with the most intrusive prompt?
In this type of prompt, you steer a student to complete a task by placing your hand on their elbow.
Gestures, positional, and looking at the targeted response are all examples of:
Discrete Trial Training (DTT) is characterized by:
options: Clear beginnings and ends with repeated trials
Allowing the student to lead the entire session

questions.prompt: Which verbal operant involves a speaker repeating what someone else said?
A little boy looks at his stuffed animal and says “Zebra!” This is an example of a:
A "Mand" is essentially a:
Natural Environment Training (NET) focuses on:
options: Sitting at a desk for 3 hours
Using the learner's current motivation in natural situations

questions.prompt: The breakdown of a task into its individual components and steps is:
In Forward Chaining, the learner is taught:
options: Every step at the same time
The first step in the sequence first

questions.prompt: Which chaining method has the learner complete only the final step independently first?
What is true about shaping?
options: Gradual improvements toward the terminal behavior are reinforced
Often used to teach a task with multiple steps

questions.prompt: What is meant by the term “terminal goal” when discussing shaping?
options: The small steps toward a new behavior

questions.prompt: Which of the following might be a successive approximation of a student asking for a toy?
options: The student grabs the toy off the shelf
The student says, “I need to go to the bathroom.”

questions.prompt: Maintenance refers to:
options: Performing a skill with different people
Continued performance of a behavior over time after training ends

questions.prompt: "Pairing" in a clinical setting involves:
options: Associating the therapist with preferred items and fun

questions.prompt: Stimulus Generalization occurs when:
options: A learner only responds to one specific person
A learner performs a skill across different people, settings, or materials
A learner requires a prompt to respond

questions.prompt: "Errorless Learning" usually involves:
options: Letting the child make 3 mistakes before helping
Prompting immediately to prevent an error

questions.prompt: Which is an example of an Intraverbal?
options: Saying "Dog" when you see a dog
Saying "Bark" when someone says "A dog goes..."

questions.prompt: "Frequency" in data collection refers to:
options: How many times a behavior happens
The time between instruction and response

questions.prompt: Frequency and rate are often used interchangeably. Which statement is correct?
options: Frequency is more accurate than rate
Rate has greater reliability than frequency
Rate refers to frequency with the addition of a time component
Only the rate is event recording

questions.prompt: Measuring the time from the start of a behavior to the end is:
You are recording the time it takes from the presentation of the demand (SD) to the first instance of behavior. What are you measuring?
Which measurement requires the behavior to occur throughout the entire interval?
Partial Interval Recording tends to:
options: Only be used for long behaviors

questions.prompt: A preference assessment where items are not replaced once chosen is:
The most common form of graph used in ABA is:
What is the primary role of an RBT?
options: Implement the plan written by the BCBA

questions.prompt: What do you need to do at the very minimum to be sure you’re ready for a successful session?
options: A cup of coffee is all you need
You should know what you are measuring and have the materials required before session starts

questions.prompt: When working with a client, whose preferences should take priority?
Which of the following is the best example of maintaining client dignity?
options: Sharing a client’s private information with other RBTs
Explaining to the waitress that your client has autism
Not restricting the client’s right to food or water
Sharing a client’s information with a peer

questions.prompt: What does HIPAA protect?
Professional boundaries include avoiding "Dual Relationships," which means:
options: Working with two clients at once
Being both a therapist and a friend/babysitter to the family

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the best way to communicate treatment progress with parents?
options: “We changed her reinforcement schedule from a FR5 to a VR3 today.”
“The client’s behavior was really bad today.”
“Today we worked on brushing her teeth. She successfully applied the toothpaste and brushed by herself for 30 seconds.”

questions.prompt: Your students are taking a test, and you write “Remember, no talking on the board”. During the test, you hear a noise and you point to the note on the board. What types of prompting are you using? Select all that apply:
